finding: CLI writes canonical YYYYMMDD-HHMM-<full_sid>.<kind>.md but the TUI still writes opencode-recovery-<sid[:8]>-<YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS>.<kind>.md and uses .compact-prompt.md. Same artifacts, two schemes/precisions/sid-lengths by entry point; 1.1.0 widened a pre-existing divergence and the migration does not normalize TUI output.
column13: Route TUI writes through canonical_recovery_name (full sid, .prompt.md); fix button label.

finding: Compact-prompt written as *.prompt.md (3692) but CLI picker looks for *.compact-prompt.md (8907) - works only via generated_paths[-1] fallback. Migration RECOVERY_KINDS has 'prompt' not 'compact-prompt'; TUI label + docstring (4752) still say .compact-prompt.md.
column11: Fix the lookup string to .prompt.md and the docstring; unify TUI to .prompt.md.

finding: parse_recovery_name matches suffix case-sensitively, so on macOS (supported CI target) a *.RESTART.MD legacy file is silently unrecognized/unmigrated. Same root as edge-cases EC-5.
proposed_action: Adopt the edge-cases case-insensitive parse fix (shared; execute once).

finding: (Positive) canonical names contain no Windows-illegal chars: safe_filename allow-list [A-Za-z0-9._-] and timestamp has no ':'. No reserved-name (CON/PRN/NUL) guard but unreachable for ses_ ids.
proposed_action: Add a Windows-valid-filename regression test; do NOT add a reserved-name filter (deferred).

finding: New config keys filter_max_bytes/filter_secret_scan (from sibling IPDs) must default safely so an existing/minimal ocman.toml keeps working and an absent key is not an error.
proposed_action: Ensure load_ocman_config returns documented defaults for absent keys; test old config loads.

finding: (Positive) parse_recovery_name reads both legacy on-disk forms plus canonical, so files written by older ocman stay readable/migratable.
proposed_action: Pin with an explicit backward-compat test.
